## SECTION 4 — Structural Diagrams & Schematics

### 🗺️ Diagram 1 — Hybrid Vehicle System Architecture (Block-Level Functional Flow)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[ICE Engine 2ZR-FXE] --> B[Planetary Power Split Device]
    B --> C[MG1 Generator]
    B --> D[MG2 Traction Motor]
    C --> E[Inverter DC-AC]
    D --> E
    E --> F[Boost Converter]
    F --> G[HV Battery Pack 244.8 V]
    G --> F
    E --> H[DC-DC Converter]
    H --> I[Auxiliary 12 V Battery]
    I --> J[Body ECU Cluster Lighting]
    G --> K[Battery ECU]
    E --> L[Inverter ECU]
    A --> M[Engine ECU]
    K --> N[HV Master ECU]
    L --> N
    M --> N
    N --> O[OBD-II Port]
    N --> P[CAN Bus Network]
```

> [!NOTE]
> **Reading the diagram:** The *Power Split Device* (planetary gearset) is the heart of Toyota's THS-II — it allows **continuous variable power split** between ICE, MG1, and MG2 without a conventional gearbox. The **Boost Converter** steps 244 V → 650 V for the MG2 to achieve higher RPM/power density.

---

### 🗺️ Diagram 2 — Hybrid Troubleshooting Decision Tree

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    startA([Customer Reports Hybrid Issue]) --> q1{Master Warning Lamp ON?}
    q1 -- Yes --> s1[Read DTCs via OEM Scan Tool]
    q1 -- No --> q2{Vehicle Enters EV Mode?}
    q2 -- No --> s2[Check 12 V Auxiliary Battery]
    q2 -- Yes --> q3{Range Reduced?}
    q3 -- Yes --> s3[Perform HV Battery SoH Test]
    q3 -- No --> q4{Engine Runs Rough?}
    q4 -- Yes --> s4[Standard ICE Tune-up]
    q4 -- No --> q5{Regen Brake Warning?}
    q5 -- Yes --> s5[Scan ABS ECU + Bleed Brakes]
    q5 -- No --> q6{Cooling Fans Always ON?}
    q6 -- Yes --> s6[Check Inverter Coolant + Fan Relay]
    q6 -- No --> endA([Capture Freeze Frame Data + Refer to OEM Manual])
    s1 --> iso1[De-energise HV System First]
    iso1 --> iso2[Wait 10 min for capacitor discharge]
    iso2 --> iso3[Wear Class-0 HV Gloves + Face Shield]
    iso3 --> meas[Use CAT-III 1000 V Meter]
```

---

### 🗺️ Diagram 3 — HV Battery Pack Diagnostic Sub-Routine

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    batA([Open HV Battery Access Panel]) --> vChk[Scan Individual Cell Voltages]
    vChk --> qDelta{Delta V Greater Than 0.3 V?}
    qDelta -- Yes --> weakCell[Identify Weak Cell Number]
    qDelta -- No --> qTemp{Cooling Fan Operational?}
    qTemp -- No --> fanFix[Clean Fan and Replace Filter]
    qTemp -- Yes --> qCorr{Corrosion on Bus Bars?}
    qCorr -- Yes --> corrFix[Torque to Spec + Apply Anti-Corrosion Gel]
    qCorr -- No --> balTest[Run OEM Cell Balancing Procedure]
    balTest --> sohTest[Measure Pack SoH]
    sohTest --> qSoh{SoH Below 70 percent?}
    qSoh -- Yes --> replBat[Recommend Battery Reconditioning or Replacement]
    qSoh -- No --> okBat([Return to Service + Log Data])
```
<!-- SECTION_4_END -->

<!-- SECTION_5_START -->
## SECTION 5 — KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

---

### 📝 PART A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

**Q1. [KTU University Exam — Dec 2023, Model Paper]**
*Define "State of Health (SoH)" of a hybrid high-voltage battery. State the typical industry replacement threshold.*

**Model Answer (3 marks):**
- SoH is defined as the ratio of the *measured usable capacity* to the *rated original capacity*, expressed as a percentage:
$$SoH = \frac{C_{measured}}{C_{rated}} \times 100\%$$
- It indicates the **degradation level** of the HV battery relative to its new condition.
- The industry-accepted **replacement threshold** is when SoH falls **below 70 %** (some OEMs use 60 % for NiMH, 80 % for Li-ion PHEV).

---

**Q2. [KTU University Exam — May 2024, Supplementary]**
*List any **three** mandatory safety precautions a technician must follow before working on the high-voltage system of a hybrid vehicle.*

**Model Answer (3 marks — 1 mark each):**
1. **De-energise** the HV system by removing the **service plug / disconnect switch** and applying a **lockout-tagout (LOTO)** device.
2. **Wait 5 to 10 minutes** after power-off to allow the inverter's internal DC-link capacitors to self-discharge below **60 V DC**.
3. **Wear personal protective equipment** — Class-0 (1000 V) insulated gloves, arc-flash face shield, and use only **CAT-III 1000 V** rated tools and multimeters.

---

### 📝 PART B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks — Internal Choice)

> [!IMPORTANT]
> KTU 2024 Scheme rule: *Each Part B question carries 14 marks split into (a) 7 marks + (b) 7 marks. You must attempt either **Question A** OR **Question B** in full.*

---

#### ✏️ QUESTION A (14 Marks)

**[KTU University Exam — Dec 2023, Modified]**

**(a)** With a neat block diagram, explain the **systematic diagnostic procedure** for **HV battery degradation** in a Toyota THS-II hybrid. *(7 marks)*

**Model Solution:**

A Toyota THS-II hybrid uses a 28-cell NiMH pack rated at 244.8 V. The diagnostic sequence is:

1. **Customer symptom capture** — reduced EV range, master warning lamp, or code P0A80. (1 mark)
2. **Visual inspection** — open HV battery access panel, check cooling fan for dust, inspect coolant level and colour, look for white residue indicating electrolyte leakage. (1 mark)
3. **DTC retrieval** with Techstream scan tool. Read codes from HV-ECU and Battery-ECU. Freeze-frame data: capture SoC, cell voltage spread, and battery temperature. (1 mark)
4. **Cell voltage scan** — measure each of the 28 cell voltages using OEM diagnostic mode. Compare against spec; flag any cell outside the nominal **7.2 – 8.4 V** band. If $\Delta V_{cell} > 0.3\ \text{V}$, identify the weak cell. (2 marks)
5. **Capacity test (SoH)** — fully charge then discharge at 1 C through OEM service procedure. Calculate using $SoH = (C_{measured}/C_{rated}) \times 100\%$. (1 mark)
6. **Decision** — SoH $\geq$ 70 %: re-balance cells and clean cooling. SoH $<$ 70 %: recommend pack reconditioning or module-level replacement. (1 mark)

> [!NOTE]
> **Block diagram** (draw in answer sheet — paper-safe ASCII if needed):

```
[Customer Symptom] → [Visual Check] → [DTC Read] → [Cell V Scan] → [SoH Test] → [Decision]
```

**(b)** Discuss the **troubleshooting of regenerative braking faults** in hybrid vehicles. Include at least **two DTC codes** and their meaning. *(7 marks)*

**Model Solution:**

Regenerative braking recovers kinetic energy via MG2 (acting as generator) and stores it in the HV battery. The brake-by-wire / electro-hydraulic system coordinates friction brakes and regen.

**Common Fault Modes:**
1. **No regen — brake pedal feels normal but fuel economy drops.** Cause: HV battery full (SoC $\geq$ 80 %) or cold battery ($-$10 °C). The ECU disables regen to protect the pack. (1 mark)
2. **Excessive friction brake use / squeal.** Cause: Regen disabled due to **DTC C1241** (low HV battery voltage) or **C1242** (regen cooperative control abnormal). (1 mark)
3. **"Wooden" brake pedal feel after brake job.** Cause: Air in brake lines because the workshop forgot to **scan-tool-bleed the ABS actuator** post-service. (1 mark)
4. **DTC P0C27** — Hybrid battery pack coolant pump performance issue. Affects regen because cold pack cannot accept charge. (1 mark)
5. **DTC P0A1B** — Hybrid vehicle control system — generator control malfunction. MG1 cannot feed MG2 efficiently, regen throughput drops. (1 mark)

**Diagnostic Steps:**
- Confirm brake pedal stroke and vacuum/electric booster function. (0.5 mark)
- Read ABS-ECU and HV-ECU DTCs; check freeze-frame data. (0.5 mark)
- Verify battery SoC and temperature; condition pack before retesting. (0.5 mark)
- Perform road test with scan tool live-data on regen current (MG2 torque should go negative during deceleration). (0.5 mark)

#### ✏️ QUESTION B (14 Marks) — *Alternative Choice*

**[KTU University Exam — May 2024, Model Paper]**

**(a)** With a neat block diagram, describe the **complete hybrid vehicle powertrain architecture** and the function of each major subsystem. *(7 marks)*

**Model Solution:**

A full hybrid (e.g., Toyota THS-II) powertrain contains the following subsystems:

1. **Internal Combustion Engine (ICE)** — usually an Atkinson-cycle engine (e.g., 2ZR-FXE 1.8 L) optimised for thermal efficiency when paired with an electric assist. (1 mark)
2. **Planetary Power Split Device** — replaces a conventional gearbox; allows continuous variable split of ICE torque between MG1 and the wheels. (1 mark)
3. **MG1 (Starter-Generator)** — starts the ICE, acts as generator to charge HV battery, and regulates the power split. (1 mark)
4. **MG2 (Traction Motor)** — primary EV propulsion, also harvests regen energy during braking. (1 mark)
5. **Inverter + Boost Converter** — converts DC from HV battery to 3-phase AC for MGs; steps 244 V up to 650 V for high-speed MG2 operation. (1 mark)
6. **HV Traction Battery** — 244.8 V NiMH (28 cells) or Li-ion (96 modules in newer models). (1 mark)
7. **DC-DC Converter + 12 V Auxiliary Battery** — steps HV down to 12 V for body electronics. (1 mark)

**Block Diagram** (to draw):

```
[ICE] → [Planetary] → [MG1] ↔ [Inverter/Boost] ↔ [HV Battery]
              ↓                  ↕
           [Wheels]            [MG2] → [Wheels]
                                  ↕
                              [DC-DC] → [12V Bat]
```

**(b)** Explain the **diagnostic procedure for inverter-coolant temperature faults**. Mention any two DTCs and their triggers. *(7 marks)*

**Model Solution:**

The inverter generates significant heat; in most hybrids it is liquid-cooled by a shared loop with the HV battery. Over-temperature triggers **derating** (reduced propulsion power) and ultimately **limp mode**.

**Diagnostic Procedure:**
1. **Verify complaint** — driver reports reduced power, "turtle" indicator, or master warning. (1 mark)
2. **Read DTCs** — common codes:
   - **P0A78** — Drive Motor "A" Inverter Over Temperature.
   - **P0A7F** — Hybrid Battery Pack Deterioration (often co-reported with cooling faults).
   - **P0AC0** — Hybrid Powertrain Control Module — System Voltage High/Low (sometimes tied to thermal derating). (2 marks)
3. **Inspect cooling system** — check coolant reservoir level, look for pink/orange residue around inverter hoses, verify the **inverter coolant pump** runs on key-on. (1 mark)
4. **Measure temperatures** — use IR thermometer or scan-tool PID (parameter) for inverter IGBT temperature; compare against ambient + load curve. Spec: inverter should not exceed **105 °C** continuously. (1 mark)
5. **Test fan and pump relays** — command ON via scan tool; if no response, check relay coil resistance and 12 V supply. (1 mark)
6. **Refill and bleed** with OEM-specified coolant (e.g., Toyota Super Long Life Coolant). Air pockets mimic overheating. (1 mark)

> [!WARNING]
> **Examiner's Pitfall (KTU 2024 Valuation Pattern):**
> Students commonly lose marks for:
> 1. ❌ Forgetting the **5–10 minute capacitor discharge wait** after switching off.
> 2. ❌ Writing DTC codes without explaining **trigger conditions**.
> 3. ❌ Confusing **MG1 and MG2 roles** in a THS-II system.
> 4. ❌ Skipping the **12 V auxiliary battery** check before diving into HV diagnostics.
> 5. ❌ Not stating the **SoH replacement threshold** (70 %) in battery questions.

## 🧾 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember (Rapid Revision Checklist)

> [!TIP]
> **Last-Minute Revision — Read this 3 times before the exam.**

- ✅ Hybrid = **ICE + MG1 + MG2 + HV Battery + Inverter + DC-DC + Boost Converter + 12 V Battery + Hybrid ECU**.
- ✅ Toyota THS-II uses a **planetary gearset** for continuous variable power split — no conventional gearbox.
- ✅ Common HV system voltages to remember: **144 V** (mild), **201.6 V** (Honda IMA), **244.8 V** (Toyota NiMH), **650 V** (boosted DC-link), **800 V** (modern BEV/PHEV).
- ✅ **State of Health (SoH)** threshold for replacement: **$\leq$ 70 %** (industry standard).
- ✅ **Insulation resistance safety**: must be $\geq 100\ \text{k}\Omega/\text{V}$ of nominal system voltage.
- ✅ **Safety sequence**: remove service plug → wait 5–10 min → verify zero potential with CAT-III meter → wear Class-0 gloves → use insulated tools.
- ✅ **Top 6 DTCs to memorise**:
  - `P0A80` — Replace HV Battery Pack
  - `P0A78` — Inverter Over-Temperature
  - `P0A7F` — Hybrid Battery Deterioration
  - `P0AC0` — HV System Voltage Issue
  - `C1241` / `C1242` — Regen / ABS Cooperative Fault
  - `U0100` — Lost Communication with ECM/PCM
- ✅ **First-check rule**: Always **load-test the 12 V auxiliary battery** before deep HV diagnostics — fixes ~30 % of "phantom" hybrid complaints.
- ✅ **Regen efficiency**: 30 – 60 % of kinetic energy recovered; reduces brake-pad wear by 30 – 50 %.
- ✅ **Cooling fan clogged** = root cause of 70 % of Prius Gen-3 HV battery faults.
- ✅ **After any HV component replacement**: clear DTCs → run **drive cycle** → perform **insulation resistance test** → certify road-safe.
- ✅ Examiner rewards: **sequence clarity**, **safety awareness**, **numeric example with units**, **block diagrams**, **correct DTC naming**.
